ab: djavadjavay vuvu, ti kadruy aken, tuki maqati aken a kivadaq itjanusun?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ui, ika makuda.
ljakua, ika makudakuda a ku kineljang.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: izua su tenget nu pasuvililj ta cengelj?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: ui i tjumaq aken.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: nasakivadaq aken tjanusun a pakatua milimilingan nua paiwan. tuki izua su tenget nu kadjaman?
ch: A:你明天早上有空嗎?我想問您關於排灣族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Paiwan people.

ab: neka aken i tjumaq yu. uri vaik amen a kivaqu nutiaw.
ch: B:明天我們要去山上採小米，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: naselangeda aken tu liaw a su kineljakeljang a pakatua kakudanan nua paiwan. tuki maqati aken a kivadaq tjanusun?
ch: A:我聽說您對排灣族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Paiwan people, so I would like to ask you something.

ab: ui, ljakua pangetjezu anga nu sikatjelu a kadjamadjaman.
ch: B:好的，但是，請你後天早上再來。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: tuki maqati aken a semasing tjanusun a seman iga?
ch: A:不知是否我能錄影製作成影片?
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ui, maqati inikamakuda.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: vuvu, pida anga su cavilj tucu?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: siva a puluq saka alu anga ku cavilj.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: vuvu, aku tarivak anan a su kinacavacavan, ika makeljang a su cavilj.
ch: A:vuvu,您很健康喔,看不出您的年紀。
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ini lja! ramaleng anga ken, neka anga ku djuludjuluin.
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了，很多事沒辦法做了。
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: na kemasinu sun vuvu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemasi kuvaleng aken.
en: B: I’m from Gu Fa Leng.

ab: paiwan a su cekelj uta?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是排灣族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Paiwan?

ab: ini, sepiuma timadju.
en: B: No, she’s a Puyuma.

ab: izua anan a su kinacaqu a zuma a kai?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: ui, macaqu aken ta na Amis kata na sepiuma
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak Aramaic and Puyuma.

ab: vuvu, naqemaljup sun kasicuayan?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ui, ru qaljup aken kasicuayan, qenetji liaw anga ku qinaljup a sacemel.
ch: B:是的。我以前經常上山打獵,請看這裡有很多的獵物。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: masalu aravac tua su tjaucikel kasauni, liaw anga ku sinivecik.
ch: A:vuvu,非常感謝您今天的分享，我做了很多記錄。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: aicu a ku sinitjaucikel kasauni, ku linangedan a kemasi ta ramaljemaljeng a tja kakudan kasicuayan.
ch: B:我剛才所説的，是祖先所流傳的文化。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: vuvu aianga su luljayan, tjaljumaza a ku sikivadaq tjanusun.
ch: A:vuvu今天辛苦您了，今天的訪談先到這裡。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: inikamakuda, ljakua namaqusaw sun?
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: ini, uri vaik anga aken, maqati aken a lemangeda ta su tjaucikel a milimilingan nu tjaivililj?
ch: A:不了,我要離開了，我下次可以聽到更多的故事嗎?
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: ui! pangetjezu anga nu tjaivililj, kigalju anga i djalan.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
